title: dombeya erythroxylon
intro: Dombeya erythroxylon, also known as the 'Bissey' or 'Hairy-stemmed Dombeya', is a plant species belonging to the family Sterculiaceae. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly in its bark, which is used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues. The bark is rich in flavonoids and phenolic compounds that have been shown to have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, offering health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and promoting wound healing. In traditional herbal medicine, the bark is often prepared as a decoction or tincture to treat a range of ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions.
